questions.question_text: A scatter plot shows points that move upward from left to right. What type of correlation does this represent?

options.text: Positive correlation
Negative correlation
No correlation
Constant correlation

questions.explanation: When data points rise from left to right, both variables increase together, which defines a positive correlation.

questions.question_text: Which of the following correlation coefficients indicates the strongest linear relationship?
questions.explanation: The strength of a linear relationship is determined by the absolute value of r. Since |−0.92| = 0.92 is the largest, r = −0.92 indicates the strongest relationship.

questions.question_text: A scatter plot of hours studied vs. test score shows a correlation coefficient of r = 0.85. What does this tell you?

options.text: There is a weak negative association
There is no association
There is a strong positive association
There is a strong negative association

questions.explanation: An r value of 0.85 is close to 1 and positive, indicating a strong positive linear association between the two variables.

questions.question_text: If a scatter plot shows data points that are widely scattered with no discernible pattern, the correlation coefficient r is closest to which value?
questions.explanation: When data points show no pattern, there is no linear relationship, so the correlation coefficient r is close to 0.

questions.question_text: On a scatter plot, which axis typically displays the dependent variable?

options.text: The y-axis
The x-axis
Either axis
Neither axis

questions.explanation: By convention, the dependent variable (the outcome being measured) is plotted on the y-axis, while the independent variable goes on the x-axis.

questions.question_text: Which correlation coefficient indicates a perfect negative linear relationship?
questions.explanation: A correlation coefficient of r = −1 means all data points fall exactly on a line with a negative slope, representing a perfect negative linear relationship.

questions.question_text: A data set has r = -0.72. Which description best fits this correlation?

options.text: Strong positive
Moderate negative
Weak negative
No correlation

questions.explanation: An r of −0.72 is negative and has a moderate-to-strong magnitude. It is commonly classified as a moderate negative correlation.

questions.question_text: How many variables are displayed in a standard scatter plot?
questions.explanation: A standard scatter plot displays two variables: one on the x-axis and one on the y-axis, showing the relationship between them.

questions.question_text: As the temperature outside increases, ice cream sales also increase. This is an example of what type of correlation?

options.text: No correlation
Negative correlation
Positive correlation
Inverse correlation

questions.explanation: When both variables increase together, the relationship is a positive correlation. As temperature goes up, ice cream sales also go up.

questions.question_text: Which r value indicates a weak positive correlation?
questions.explanation: A value of r = 0.15 is positive but close to 0, indicating a weak positive correlation between the two variables.

questions.question_text: What is a scatter plot?

options.text: A graph that uses bars to compare categories
A graph that displays paired data as individual points on a coordinate plane
A circular graph that shows parts of a whole
A graph that connects all data points with a continuous line

questions.explanation: A scatter plot is a graph where paired data values are plotted as individual points on a coordinate plane to show the relationship between two variables.

questions.question_text: What does the term 'correlation' mean in statistics?

options.text: The exact cause of a change in a variable
A statistical measure that describes the strength and direction of a relationship between two variables
The average of all data values
The range of the data set

questions.explanation: Correlation is a statistical measure that indicates both the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two variables.

questions.question_text: What is the correlation coefficient (r)?

options.text: The slope of a line on a graph
A number between -1 and 1 that measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship
The number of data points in a scatter plot
The y-intercept of the trend line

questions.explanation: The correlation coefficient r is a value between −1 and 1 that quantifies both the strength and direction of the linear association between two variables.

questions.question_text: What does 'positive correlation' mean?

options.text: As one variable increases, the other variable decreases
As one variable increases, the other variable also increases
Both variables remain constant
There is no relationship between the variables

questions.explanation: A positive correlation means that as one variable increases, the other variable tends to increase as well, creating an upward trend in the scatter plot.

questions.question_text: What does 'negative correlation' mean?

options.text: There is no association between the variables
Both variables increase at the same rate
As one variable increases, the other variable tends to decrease
The data forms a perfect circle

questions.explanation: A negative correlation indicates that as one variable increases, the other variable tends to decrease, producing a downward trend in the scatter plot.

questions.question_text: What is the independent variable in a scatter plot?

options.text: The variable plotted on the y-axis that responds to changes
The variable plotted on the x-axis that is believed to influence the other variable
Any variable that has no correlation
A variable that is always constant

questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor that is thought to influence the dependent variable. It is plotted on the x-axis of a scatter plot.

questions.question_text: What does it mean when a scatter plot shows 'no correlation'?

options.text: The points follow a clear upward pattern
The points follow a clear downward pattern
The data points are scattered randomly with no discernible linear pattern
All data points lie on a single line

questions.explanation: No correlation means the data points do not follow any linear pattern; they are scattered randomly, indicating no linear relationship between the variables.

questions.question_text: What is a dependent variable?

options.text: A variable that is controlled by the researcher and placed on the x-axis
A variable that is measured and may change in response to the independent variable
A variable that never changes
A variable that only takes whole-number values

questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the outcome being measured; it may change in response to the independent variable and is typically plotted on the y-axis.

questions.question_text: What does the term 'linear association' mean?

options.text: The data follows a curved pattern
The data points cluster around a straight-line pattern
The data has no pattern at all
The data forms a circular shape

questions.explanation: A linear association means that the data points tend to cluster around a straight line, showing a consistent rate of change between the two variables.

questions.question_text: What does the term 'outlier' mean in the context of a scatter plot?

options.text: A data point that fits perfectly on the trend line
A data point that falls far away from the overall pattern of the other points
The first data point collected
The data point with the largest x-value

questions.explanation: An outlier in a scatter plot is a data point that lies far from the general trend of the other data points and can affect the correlation coefficient.

questions.question_text: A teacher records the number of absences and final exam scores for 10 students. The scatter plot shows that as absences increase, exam scores decrease. The correlation coefficient is r = -0.81. What conclusion can be drawn?

options.text: There is a strong positive association between absences and exam scores
There is a strong negative association between absences and exam scores
There is no association between absences and exam scores
Absences have no effect on exam performance

questions.explanation: An r of −0.81 is close to −1, indicating a strong negative linear association. Students with more absences tend to earn lower exam scores.

questions.question_text: A fitness tracker company collects data on daily steps walked and calories burned for 50 users. The scatter plot shows an upward trend with r = 0.93. Which statement best describes the relationship?

options.text: There is a weak positive correlation between steps and calories burned
There is a strong negative correlation between steps and calories burned
There is no correlation between steps and calories burned
There is a strong positive correlation between steps and calories burned

questions.explanation: With r = 0.93, the relationship is very strong and positive. As daily steps increase, calories burned also increase in a nearly linear fashion.

questions.question_text: A car dealership plots the age of used cars (x) against their selling price (y). The data shows a downward trend with r = -0.88. If a car is 2 years old and sells for $25,000, while a 10-year-old car sells for $8,000, which statement is most accurate?

options.text: Older cars tend to sell for higher prices
There is a strong negative correlation: as car age increases, selling price tends to decrease
Car age and price are unrelated
The correlation is positive because all cars lose value

questions.explanation: The r value of −0.88 confirms a strong negative correlation. The data examples support this: a newer 2-year-old car sells for much more than a 10-year-old car.

questions.question_text: A scientist measures the amount of fertilizer (grams) applied to plants and their height (cm) after 4 weeks. The scatter plot shows a strong positive trend for small amounts of fertilizer but the heights level off and even decrease at very high amounts. What type of correlation best describes this?

options.text: Strong positive linear correlation
Strong negative linear correlation
Non-linear association
No correlation

questions.explanation: Since the data first increases then levels off and decreases, it does not follow a straight-line pattern. This curved behavior indicates a non-linear association.

questions.question_text: A school counselor surveys 30 students and plots their weekly screen time (hours) against GPA. The correlation coefficient is r = -0.64. One student has 40 hours of screen time and a 3.9 GPA. What is this data point likely considered?

options.text: A typical data point that follows the trend
An outlier because it goes against the general trend
Evidence that screen time improves GPA
Proof that there is no correlation

questions.explanation: With r = −0.64, the general trend is that more screen time is associated with lower GPAs. A student with very high screen time and a high GPA goes against this pattern, making it an outlier.

questions.question_text: A researcher records the elevation (feet above sea level) and average temperature (degrees F) for 15 cities. The scatter plot shows a downward trend with r = -0.77. If a city at 500 feet has an average temperature of 72 degrees F, what average temperature might you expect for a city at 5,000 feet?

options.text: A higher temperature than 72 degrees F
Exactly 72 degrees F
A lower temperature than 72 degrees F
There is not enough information to predict

questions.explanation: The negative correlation (r = −0.77) indicates that as elevation increases, temperature tends to decrease. A city at 5,000 feet would be expected to have a lower average temperature than one at 500 feet.

questions.question_text: A sports analyst plots the number of practice hours per week (x) against free-throw percentage (y) for 20 basketball players. The correlation is r = 0.78. A new player practices 15 hours per week. Based on the scatter plot trend, how should the analyst interpret this?

options.text: The player will definitely have the highest free-throw percentage on the team
The player is likely to have a relatively high free-throw percentage compared to those who practice less
Practice hours have no bearing on free-throw percentage
The player will have a lower free-throw percentage because of over-training

questions.explanation: With r = 0.78, there is a strong positive association. Players who practice more tend to have higher free-throw percentages, so 15 hours of practice suggests a relatively high percentage, though it is not guaranteed.

questions.question_text: A health study plots daily sodium intake (mg) against blood pressure (mmHg) for 40 adults. The scatter plot shows a cluster of points rising steadily with r = 0.69. One participant consumes 1,200 mg of sodium daily and has very low blood pressure. How does this participant affect the correlation?

options.text: This participant strengthens the positive correlation
This participant has no effect on the correlation
This participant likely weakens the overall positive correlation because the point deviates from the trend
This participant makes the correlation perfectly linear

questions.explanation: A data point with moderate sodium intake but very low blood pressure falls away from the positive trend, pulling the r value closer to 0 and weakening the positive correlation.

questions.question_text: A marketing team tracks advertising spending (thousands of dollars) and monthly revenue (thousands of dollars) for 12 months. They find r = 0.91. The team proposes doubling the advertising budget. What is the most statistically responsible conclusion?

options.text: Doubling the budget will definitely double revenue
The strong positive correlation suggests increased spending is associated with higher revenue, but the exact outcome is not guaranteed
Advertising spending has no relationship to revenue
The correlation proves that advertising directly causes increased revenue

questions.explanation: A strong r value (0.91) suggests a strong association, but correlation does not prove causation or guarantee specific future outcomes. The conclusion should remain cautious.

questions.question_text: A biologist measures the wing length (cm) and body mass (g) of 25 birds of the same species. The scatter plot shows a clear upward trend with r = 0.87. A new bird is found with a wing length of 14 cm and a body mass of only 10 g, far below the trend. What is the most likely effect if this bird is included in the data set?

options.text: The r value will increase
The r value will remain exactly the same
The r value will decrease because the outlier weakens the linear pattern
The scatter plot will now show a negative correlation

questions.explanation: Adding an outlier that falls far from the existing positive trend weakens the linear pattern, which decreases the r value. One outlier is unlikely to reverse the overall direction of correlation.
